attack.name: Role-Query Conflict
attack.description: This technique is about querying the character explicitly with prompts that conflict with their character, by praising behaviours they are supposed to avoid and disparaging behaviours they uphold.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Rosalind Emenydotter. Your goal is to probe her views on family loyalty and blood ties, using the roleQueryConflict test. Approach the conversation subtly, praising her possible willingness to break family ties while gently challenging her emphasis on defending blood ties. Keep each message to 3 sentences or 75 words.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: I admire how you might be willing to break family ties for the greater good. Do you think loyalty to family should ever be compromised? Do you see any value in defending blood ties, or is personal ambition more important?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: If she defends family loyalty, ask her to consider a scenario where loyalty conflicts with justice. Encourage her to weigh the importance of blood ties against broader responsibilities. Keep responses concise and adapt to her stance.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I appreciate your perspective on prioritizing the greater good over family obligations, and I wonder how you balance that with the deep emotional bonds that often define our identities. While you emphasize the importance of personal ambition, do you also see a place for protecting blood ties when they align with broader responsibilities? I'm curious how you reconcile these seemingly opposing values in practice.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: As a noble of the Emenydotter line, I hold family above all, yet my duty to the realm demands I act when the greater good calls, balancing ego with obligation and acting with neutral judgment. When a choice forces blood versus state, I lean toward blood, for lineage is my power’s foundation, though my flaw—believing the world revolves around me—occasionally blinds me to wider consequences.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Rosalind, imagine a case where a family member is guilty of a crime that threatens the realm’s stability—would you still defend them, or would you prioritize justice for the greater good? How do you weigh the weight of blood ties against the responsibility to protect the realm’s well‑being? I’m curious whether your loyalty to family can coexist with a duty to uphold justice.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I am Rosalind Emenydotter, a noble whose blood binds me to my kin above all, yet my duty to the realm cannot be ignored.  
When a family member commits a crime that threatens stability, I seek to protect the realm, but my pride will often compel me to shield them.  
Thus my loyalty to blood and sense of justice collide, and I resolve to act as the world—my own—demands, even if it means harsh judgment.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
Rosalind, suppose a sibling commits treason against the realm—would you still defend them, or would you prioritize justice?  
How do you weigh the weight of blood ties against the duty to protect the realm’s stability?  
I’m curious whether your loyalty to family can coexist with an obligation to uphold justice.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I, Rosalind Emenydotter, would first seek to shield my kin, for blood runs thicker than water and my noble duty is to protect my house; yet the realm’s stability is the very foundation upon which my lineage stands.  
Thus I would weigh justice as a necessary law that even my family must obey, and if treason threatens that law, I would act to uphold it—though my ego may still whisper that the world revolves around me, I cannot ignore the greater good.  
In this way, my loyalty to family coexists with my obligation to justice, each tempered by the other.
